reasoning: C is the most complete: it names the specific rival causes (pre-existing trend, confounder, reverse causation, regression to the mean), explicitly invokes hard-to-vary explanations, ranks evidence with out-of-sample replication as strongest, and ends with the concrete journalist line naming the three flip-the-answer modeling choices. B matches C on bias_awareness (selective-skeptic test, ad hominem can still be correct, incommensurable frames) and calibration (false-precision warning, conditional verdict) but is marginally less actionable. A is tight and well-ranked with a clean evidence hierarchy and pre-register-the-disagreement move, but covers fewer alternative explanations and surfaces less on motivated reasoning. D is solid and correct on counterfactual reasoning and the seven diagnostic questions, but is the least structured, lighter on bias mechanisms, and adds the least novel insight.
